title: Chocolate on Wool
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within hours; one home round max
stainChemistry: Chocolate is four-part: cocoa butter + milk casein + cocoa tannin + sugar. Wool is keratin protein. The standard cotton attack (enzyme for casein, peroxide for tannin) is BANNED on wool — enzymes digest keratin equally with casein, peroxide yellows wool, alkaline cleaners felt wool AND lock tannin. Heat felts wool AND sets the casein AND sets tannin in one bad move. Home protocol is constrained: ice-lift solid bulk, cold-water-press-blot, ONE pass with wool-safe detergent, 60-sec vinegar, then pro.
whyThisWorks: Wool specialist with cold POG (solvent for cocoa butter) + cold NSD (for casein) + timed acetic acid + dilute peroxide can lift fresh chocolate without felting. Home protocol does the bulk-lift and casein-soften with cold + Dawn-equivalent, then escalates. Repeat home rounds on wool felt the fiber.
defaultAssumption: Wool + chocolate = pro within hours. ONE careful home round. Dark chocolate harder than milk; white chocolate is mostly fat (no tannin) — slightly easier.

spottingProtocol.technique: Ice bag harden + plastic card lift
spottingProtocol.equipment: Ice in plastic bag, plastic card
spottingProtocol.instruction: Press ice bag on chocolate for 5 min to firm cocoa butter. Lift solids with flat edge of plastic card. Don't scrape wool — flat-edge lift only.
Press cold water on the BACK with vertical pressure. No rubbing — agitates wool, felts fiber. Replace cloth as it picks up brown.
Cold solvent dissolves cocoa butter without water-based agitation. Press cloth, flip frequently.
Cold NSD handles casein without enzymes. Press only, no agitation.
For residual tannin. STRICT 60-sec dwell. Flush cold immediately.

spottingProtocol.agent: Dilute Hydrogen Peroxide for Oxidized Residue
spottingProtocol.instruction: For oxidized brown, dilute peroxide 1–2 min monitored. Flush cold immediately.
Rinse until clear. Press between dry towels — never wring. Lay flat.

steps: 6. Dilute H₂O₂ for oxidized residue, 1–2 min monitored.
7. Final cold rinse, press dry flat.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — wool felts above 30°C when wet
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets casein + sets tannin
NEVER enzymes — digest keratin AND casein equally
NEVER alkaline cleaners — felt + darken tannin
60-sec ceiling on acid; 1–2 min on peroxide

homeSolutions.agent: ONE Round Max — Felt Risk Stacks With Tannin Lock
homeSolutions.instruction: Chocolate on wool stacks four failure modes: heat felts wool AND sets casein AND sets tannin AND polymerizes the cocoa butter. Alkaline cleaners felt AND lock tannin. Enzymes digest wool. Repeat-round chemistry on wool is the #1 felting cause. ONE careful round, then stop.

Press a zip-lock bag of ice on the chocolate for 5 minutes to firm the cocoa butter. Lift solid pieces off with the FLAT edge of a plastic card. Don't scrape — wool fibers shred under any sharp edge. Just lift bulk.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Press-Blot from the BACK
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn inside out. Place white towel under the stain. Press a SECOND white cloth dampened in COLD water onto the BACK. Vertical pressure only — no rubbing, no scrubbing. Cold ONLY — heat felts wool, sets casein, sets tannin.

Mix half a teaspoon of Eucalan or Soak (or plain clear Dawn) in two cups COLD water. Dip cloth, wring nearly dry, PRESS on stain. 3 minutes dwell. ONE PASS. Do NOT use OxiClean or any "bio" detergent — they contain enzymes that digest wool.

Mix 1 part white vinegar with 1 part cool water. Press a cotton swab on the stain for 60 SECONDS MAX — set a timer. Flush with cool water immediately. Vinegar handles residual tannin AND keeps it on the lighter (acid) side.

Press wool between two dry white towels — stack, press, lift. No wringing or twisting. Lay flat on fresh towel, reshape damp, air-dry at room temperature out of sun. NEVER hang wet wool. NEVER apply heat.

homeSolutions.agent: Pro Within Hours If Anything Remains
homeSolutions.instruction: Take to a wool specialist (call ahead). Tell them: "Chocolate on wool (cocoa butter + milk casein + tannin), age [hours], one round at home with ice-lift + cold + Dawn/Eucalan + 60-sec vinegar. No hot water, no enzymes, no OxiClean, no peroxide. [Residual brown / cocoa shadow]."

steps: 1. Wool + chocolate = ONE home round max.
2. Ice + flat-edge lift solid bulk. No scraping.
3. Cold water press-blot from the BACK (no rubbing).
4. One pass with Eucalan / Soak / Dawn, 3 min cold.
6. Press dry between towels, lay flat air-dry.
7. Anything remaining = pro. NO second home round.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after one careful round. Repeat home chemistry on wool felts the fiber.
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets casein + sets tannin

neverDo: Never agitate or scrub wet wool
Never enzymes — digests keratin AND casein
Never alkaline / OxiClean — felts + darkens tannin
fiberSensitivities: Wool keratin felts when wet + warm + agitated
Disulfide bonds re-cross-link irreversibly under heat
Enzymes digest keratin equally with casein

materialWarnings: ONE round home max — repeat chemistry felts wool
Four failure modes stack on this combination
Flat-edge lift only — never scrape wool

escalation.when: After one careful home round, regardless of result. Earlier for dark chocolate, large spills, or high-value garments.
escalation.whatToTell: "Chocolate on wool (cocoa butter + milk casein + tannin), age [hours], one round at home with ice-lift + cold + [wool-safe detergent] + 60-sec vinegar. No hot water, no enzymes, no peroxide. [Residual brown / cocoa shadow]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in wool (call ahead — confirm cold POG + acetic acid spotting capability)

contextQuestions: How long ago — fresh, hours, or older?
Wool type — merino / regular / felt / blend?
Has anyone used hot water / OxiClean / agitation?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Dark chocolate has more tannin — harder. White chocolate is mostly cocoa butter — slightly easier. Merino felts faster. Pre-applied enzyme = wool likely partially digested; disclose to pro.
